Nature. Hungry
Explore Concepts About Make a Difference Useful Links

References used for the Wild Shores of Singapore website

References used for this website

Abbott, R. Tucker, 1991. Seashells of South East Asia. Graham Brash, Singapore. 145 pp.

Allen, Gerald R., 1997. Tropical Marine Life. Periplus editions, Singapore. 64 pp.

Allen, Gerald R and Roger Steene. 2002. Indo-Pacific Coral Reef Field Guide. Tropical Reef Research. 378pp.

Allen, Gerry, 2000. Marine Fishes of South-East Asia: A Field Guide for Anglers and Divers. Periplus Editions. 292 pp.

Aw, Michael, 1997. Tropical Reef Life: A Marine Awareness Guide OceanEnvironment Ltd, Australia. 159 pp.

Aw, Michael, 2000 (revised edition). Tropical Reef Fishes: A ‘Getting to Know You’ and Identification GuideOceanEnvironment Ltd, Australia. 160 pp.

Baker, Nick and Kelvin Lim. 2008. Wild Animals of Singapore: A Photographic Guide to Mammals, Reptiles, Amphibians and Freshwater Fishes Vertebrate Study Group, Nature Society (Singapore). 180 pp.

Bell, Peter R. and Hemsley, Alan R. 2000. Green Plants: Their Origin and Diversity 2nd edition. Cambridge University Press. 349 pp.

Behrens, David W., 2005. Nudibranch Behavior. Second print (2007). New World Publications. USA. 176 pp.

Berrill, N. J. and Jacquelyn Berrill, 1957. 1001 Questions Answered about the Seashore Dover publications, 305 pp.

Bond, Carl E., 1996. Biology of Fishes 2nd ed. Thomson Learning Inc., 750pp.

Borneman, Eric H. 2001. Aquarium Corals: Selection, Husbandry and Natural History T.F. H Publications. 464 pp

Braack, L. E. O. Fascinating Insects of Southeast Asia Times Edition. 130pp
.
Briffett, Clive, 1986. A Guide to the Common Birds of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre, Singapore. 160 pp.

Bucknill, John A. S. & Chasen, F. N., 1927 (1990 ed.). Birds of Singapore and South-east Asia. Tynron Press, Scotland. 247 pp. s

Bunjamin Dharma. 1988. Indonesian shells (Siput dan Kerang Indonesia). PT Sarana Graha. Indonesia. 111 pp.

Burkill, I. H., 1993. A Dictionary of the Economic Products of the Malay Peninsula. 3rd printing. Publication Unit, Ministry of Agriculture, Malaysia, Kuala Lumpur. Volume 1: 1-1240; volume 2: 1241-2444.

Calumpong, H. P. & Menez, E. G., 1997.Field Guide to the Common Mangroves, Seagrasses and Algae of the Philippines. Bookmark, Inc., the Philippines. 197 pp.

Castro, Peter and Michael E Huber, 2003 (4th ed.). Marine Biology McGraw-Hill, US. 468 pp.

Chou, L. M., 1998. A Guide to the Coral Reef Life of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 128 pages.

Chou Loke Meng. 1989. Hard corals of Singapore. Reef Ecology Study Team, the National University of Singapore. A set of 4 posters.

Chua, E.K. Ours to protect: Nature in Singapore, 1993. Nature Society (Singapore), Singapore. 131pp.

Chua, E.K. Pulau Ubin: Ours to treasure, 2000. Simply Green, Singapore. 139pp.

Chuang, S. H., 1961. On Malayan Shores. Muwu Shosa, Singapore. 225 pp., plates 1-112.

Coleman, Neville. 2007. Sea stars: Echinoderms of Asia/Indo-Pacific. Neville Coleman�s Underwater Geographic Pty Ltd, Australia.136pp.

Coleman, Neville. 2003. 2002 Sea Shells: Catalogue of Indo-Pacific Mollusca Neville Coleman�s Underwater Geographic Pty Ltd, Australia.144pp.

Coleman, Neville. undated. Sea Stars of Australasia and their relatives. Neville Coleman�s World of Water, Australia. 64pp.

Coleman, Neville, 1989. Nudibranchs of the South Pacific Vol 1. 64 pp.

Coleman, Neville. 2001. 1001 Nudibranchs: Catalogue of Indo-Pacific Sea Slugs. Neville Coleman�s Underwater Geographic Pty Ltd, Australia.144pp.

Coleman, Neville. 2008. Nudibranchs Encyclopedia - Catalogue of Asia/Indo Pacific sea slugs. Neville Coleman’s World of Water, Australia. 415pp.

Corners, E. J. H., 1997. Wayside Trees of Malaya: in two volumes. Fourth edition, Malayan Nature Society, Kuala Lumpur. Volume 1: 1-476 pp, plates 1-38; volume 2: 477-861 pp., plates 139-236.

Cox, Merel J., Peter Paul van Dijk, Jarujin Nabhitabhata and Kumthorn Thirakhupt. 1998. A Photographic Guide to Snakes and Other Reptiles of Thailand, Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore* New Holland. pp 144.

Das, Indraneil. 2004. A Pocket Guide: Lizards of Borneo* Natural History Publications (Borneo). 83 pp.

Davey, Keith, 1998. A Photographic Guide to Seashore Life of Australia. New Holland, Australia.144 pp.

Davison, G.W. H. and P. K. L. Ng and Ho Hua Chew, 2008. The Singapore Red Data Book: Threatened plants and animals of Singapore. Nature Society (Singapore). 285 pp.

Davidson, G. W. H. & Chew Yen Fook, 1995. A Photographic Guide to Birds of Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore New Holland Publishers, UK. 144 pp.

Debelius, Helmut, 2001. Nudibranchs and Sea Snails: Indo-Pacific Field Guide IKAN-Unterwasserachiv, Frankfurt. 321 pp.

Debelius, Helmut, 2001. Crustacea Guide of the World: Atlantic Ocean, Indian Ocean, Pacific Ocean IKAN-Unterwasserachiv, Frankfurt. 321 pp.

Edward E. Ruppert, Richard S. Fox, Robert D. Barnes. 2004.Invertebrate Zoology Brooks/Cole of Thomson Learning Inc., 7th Edition. pp. 963

Erhardt, Harry and Daniel Knop. 2005. Corals: Indo-Pacific Field Guide IKAN-Unterwasserachiv, Frankfurt. 305 pp.

Fabricius, Katharina and Philip Alderslade, 2001. Soft Corals and Sea Fans. Australian Institute of Marine Science and the Museum and Art Gallery of the Northern Territoriy. 264 pp

Field, Colin, 1995. Journey among Mangroves. International Society for Mangrove Ecosystems, 139 pp.

Fiene-Severns, Pauline, Mike Severns and Ruth Dyerly, 2000. Periplus Nature Guides: Tropical Seashells. Periplus Editions. 64pp.

Foo Toik Shiew, 1985. Guide to the Wildflowers of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 160 pp.

Francis, Charles M. 2001. Mammals of South-East Asia New Holland Publishers. 128 pp.

Giesen, Wim and Stephan Wulffraat, Max Zieren and Liesbeth Scholten.

  1. Mangrove Guidebook for Southeast Asia (PDF online downloadable). RAP publication 2006/07 Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific Bangkok.

Gopalakrishnakone P., 1990. A Colour Guide to Dangerous Animals. Venom & Toxin Research Group, Faculty of Medicine, National University of Singapore. 156 pp.

Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens and Gary C. Williams. 1996. Coral Reef Animals of the Indo-Pacific: Animal life from Africa to Hawaii exclusive of the vertebrates Sea Challengers. 314pp.

Hails, Christopher & Frank Jarvis. 1995 (ed). Birds of Singapore. Times Edition, Singapore. 168 pp.

Hartog, C. Den. The Sea-grasses of the World, 1970. North-Holland Pub. Co., Amsterdam. 276 pp., 20 pp. of photos.

Hendler, Gordon, John E. Miller, David L. Pawson and Porter M. Kier, 1995. Sea Stars, Sea Urchins, and Allies: Echinoderms of Florida and the Caribbean. Smithsonian Institution Press. 390 pp.

Hillson, C. J. 1982. Seaweeds: A Color-Coded, Illustrated Guide to Common Marine Plants of the East Coast of the United States Keystone Books. 194pp.

Hsuan Keng, S.C. Chin and H. T. W. Tan. 1990, The Concise Flora of Singapore: Gymnosperms and Dicotyledons. Singapore University Press. 222 pp.

Hsuan Keng, S.C. Chin and H. T. W. Tan.1998, The Concise Flora of Singapore II: Monoctyledons Singapore University Press. 215 pp.

Hogarth, Peter J. 2007. The Biology of Mangroves and Seagrasses (Biology of Habitats) Oxford University Press. 273 pp.

Huisman, John M. 2000. Marine Plants of Australia University of Western Australia Press. 300pp.

Humann, Paul and Ned Deloach. 2002. Reef Creature Identification: Florida, Caribbean, Bahamas New World Publications. 420pp.

Humann, Paul and Ned Deloach. 2010. Reef Creature Identification: Tropical Pacific New World Publications. 497pp.

Jones Diana S. and Gary J. Morgan, 2002. A Field Guide to Crustaceans of Australian Waters. Reed New Holland. 224 pp.

Jones, R.E. (Ed.) et al. 2000. Polychaetes and Allies: The Southern Synthesis. Australian Biological Resources Study, Canberra. 465pp.

Kuiter, Rudie H., 2000 (English edition). Seahorses, Pipefishes and their Relatives: A Comprehensive Guide to Syngnathiformes TMC Publishing, UK. 240 pp.

Kuiter, Rudie H. 2002. Guide to Sea Fishes of Australia: A Comprehensive Reference for Divers & Fishermen New Holland Publishers. 434pp.

Kuiter, Rudie H and Helmut Debelius. 2009. World Atlas of Marine Fauna . IKAN-Unterwasserachiv. 723pp.

Koh, Esther G. L. and L. M. Chou. 1989. The Mushroom Corals of Singapore. National University of Singapore. 45pp.

Lane, David J.W. and Didier Vandenspiegel. 2003. A Guide to Sea Stars and Other Echinoderms of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 187pp.

Larson, Helen K and Kelvin K. P. Lim. 2005. A Guide to Gobies of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 164pp.

Lekagul, B. and Round, P.D. A Guide to the Birds of Thailand

  1. Saha Karn Bhaef Co.Ltd., Bangkok. 457pp.

Lieske, Ewald and Robert Myers. 2001. Coral Reef Fishes of the World Periplus Editions. 400pp.

Lim, Kelvin K. P. & Jeffrey K. Y. Low, 1998. A Guide to the Common Marine Fishes of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 163 pp.

Lim, Kelvin K. P. & Francis L K Lim, 1992. A Guide to the Amphibians and Reptiles of Singapore* Singapore Science Centre. 160 pp.

Lim, Kim Seng and Dana Gardner, 1997. An Illustrated Field Guide to the Birds of Singapore. Sun Tree Publishing Limited, Singapore. 226 pp.

Lim, S., P. Ng, L. Tan, & W. Y. Chin, 1994. Rhythm of the Sea: The Life and Times of Labrador Beach. Division of Biology, School of Science, Nanyang Technological University & Department of Zoology, the National University of Singapore. 160 pp.

Lim Swee Cheng, Nicole de Voogd and Tan Koh Siang. 2008. A Guide to Sponges of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 173pp.

Madoc, G. C., 1947 (1992 4th ed). An Introduction to Malayan Birds. The Malayan Nature Society, Kuala Lumpur. 227 pp.

Mastaller, Michael, 1997. Mangroves: The Forgotten Forest Between Land and Sea. Tropical Press, Malaysia, 200 pp.

Miskelly, Ashely. 2002. Sea Urchins of Australia and the Indo-Pacific. Capricornia Publications. 180pp.

Moore, J., 2001. An Introduction to the Invertebrates Cambridge University Press. 355 pp.

Morton, Brian & John Morton, 1983. The Sea Shore Ecology of Hong Kong. Hong Kong University Press. 350 pp.

Newman, Leslie and Lester Cannon. 2003. Marine Flatworms: The World of Polyclads. CSIRO Publishing. 97pp.

Ng, Angie et. al. 2005. A guide to the fabulous figs of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre.152 pp.

Ng, P. K. L. & Y. C. Wee, 1994. The Singapore Red Data Book: Threatened Plants and Animals of Singapore. The Nature Society (Singapore), Singapore. 343 pp.

Ng, Peter K. L. & N. Sivasothi, 1999. A Guide to the Mangroves of Singapore II (Animal Diversity). Singapore Science Centre. 168 pp.

Ng, Peter K. L. & N. Sivasothi, 1999. A Guide to the Mangroves of Singapore I (Plant Diversity). Singapore Science Centre. 168 pp.

Ng, Peter K. L. and Daniele Guinot and Peter J. F. Davie, 2008. Systema Brachyurorum: Part 1. An annotated checklist of extant Brachyuran crabs of the world. The Raffles Bulletin of Zoology. Supplement No. 17, 31 Jan 2008. 286 pp. (Online PDF on the Raffles Bulletin of Zoology website)

Ng, Peter K. L. and Wang Luan Keng and Kelvin K. P. Lim, 2008. Private Lives: An Expose of Singapore’s Mangroves. The Raffles Museum of Biodiversity Research . 249 pp.

Niesen, Thomas M., 2000. The Marine Biology Coloring Book Harpercollins Trade Sales Dept

Norman, Mark and Helmut Debelius, 2000. Cephalopods: A World Guide. ConchBooks, Germany. 319 pp

Norman, Mark and Amanda Reid. 2000. A Guide to Squid, Cuttlefish and Octopuses of Australasia The Gould League of Australia. 96pp.

Nowak, Ronald M. 2003. Walker’s Marine Mammals of the World. The Johns Hopkins University Press. 263 pp.

Nowak, Ronald M. 1994. Walker’s Bats of the World*The Johns Hopkins University Press. 287 pp.

Ong, Jin Eong & Gong, Wooi Khoon (eds.), 2001. The Encyclopedia of Malaysia (Vol. 6): The Seas Didier Millet, Malaysia. 144 pp.

Pechenik, Jan A., 2005. Biology of the Invertebrates. 5th edition. McGraw-Hill Book Co., Singapore. 578 pp.

Pham, M. N., H. T. W. Tan, S. Mitrovic & H. H. T. Yeo, 2011. A Checklist of the Algae of Singapore (pdf). Raffles Museum of Biodiversity Research, National University of Singapore, Singapore. 100 pp. Uploaded 1 August 2011.

Polunin, Ivan. 1987. Plants and Flowers of Singapore*. Times Editions. 160pp

Ripple, Jeff, 1999. Manatees and Dugongs of the World Voyageur Press, USA. 144 pp.

Robin, B and C. Petron. 2001. Living Corals. Les Editions du Pacifique. 144pp.

Schoppe, Sabine, 2000. Echinoderms of the Philippines: A guide to common shallow water sea stars, brittle sstars, sea urchins, sea cucumbers and feather stars. Times Edition, Singapore. 144 pp.

Scott, P.J.B., 1984. The corals of Hong Kong. Hong Kong University Press. 112pp.

Searle, A. G. 1980. An Illustrated Key to Malayan Hard Corals. Malayan Nature Society. 28pp 41 plates.

V. Selvam. 2007. Trees and Shrubs of the Maldives. Food and Agriculture Organisation RAP publication 2007/12.

Sprung, Julian. 1999. Corals: A Quick Reference Guide. Ricordea Publishing 240 pp.

Sparks, John, 1999. Battle of the Sexes in the Animal World BBC Worldwide, London. 224 pp.

Stafford-Deitsch, Jeremy, 1996. Mangrove: The Forgotten Habitat. Immel Publishing, London. 277 pp.

Strange, Morten & Allan Jeyarajasingam, 1999. Birds : A Photographic Guide to the Birds of Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore. Sun Tree Publishing Limited, Singapore. 258 pp.

Strange, Morten, 1998. Birds of South-East Asia: A Photographic Guide to the Birds of Thailand, Malaysia, Singapore, the Philippines and Indonesia. New Holland Publishers. 112 pp.

Strange, Morten, 2000. Tropical Birds of Malaysia and Singapore. Periplus Editions, Hong Kong. 64 pp.

Stuebing, Robert B and Robert F. Inger. 1999. A Field Guide to the Snakes of Borneo Natural History Publications (Borneo). 254 pp.

Sumich, James L. 1999. An Introduction to the Biology of Marine Life 7th ed., WCB/McGraw-Hill. 484 pp.

Tan, Hugh T.W. L.M. Chou, Darren C. J. Yeo and Peter K.L. Ng. 2007. The Natural Heritage of Singapore. Second edition. Prentice Hall. 271 pp.

Tan, Hugh T.W. and T. Morgany. 2001. A Guide to Growing the Native Plants of Singapore. BP Science Centre Guidebook. 168pp.

Tan, K. S. & L. M. Chou, 2000. A Guide to the Common Seashells of Singapore. Singapore Science Centre. 160 pp.

Tan, Leo W. H. & Ng, Peter K. L., 1988. A Guide to Seashore Life. The Singapore Science Centre, Singapore. 160 pp.

Tan, Ria and Alan Yeo. 2003. Chek Jawa Guidebook. Simply Green, 213pp.

Tan Siong Kiat and Henrietta P. M. Woo, 2010 Preliminary Checklist of The Molluscs of Singapore (pdf), Raffles Museum of Biodiversity Research, National University of Singapore.

Tee Swee Ping and Wee Mei Lynn (eds). 2001. Trees of our Garden City. National Parks Board. 202 pp.

Teo, L.W., and Wee, Y.C., Seaweeds of Singapore, 1983. Singapore University Press, Singapore. 123pp.

Tomlinson, P. B., 1986. The Botany of Mangroves Cambridge University Press. USA. 419 pp.

Trono, Gavino. C. Jr., 1997. Field Guide and Atlas of the Seaweed Resources of the Philippines.. Bookmark, Inc., the Philippines. 306 pp.

Vermeij, Geerat J. 1993. A Natural History of Shells. Princeton University Press, USA. 207pp.

Veron, Jen. 2000. Corals of the World Australian Institute of Marine Science, Australia. 3 volumes.

Waycott, Michelle (et. al). 2004. A Guide to Tropical Seagrasses of the Indo-West Pacific. 2004. James Cook University. 72 pp.

Whitmore, T. C. 1998 (2nd edition). Palms of Malaya.. White Lotus. 136 pp.

Wells, D.R. 1999. The Birds of the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Vol.1, Non-Passerines. Academic Press, London. 648 pp.

Wells, Fred E. and Clayton W. Bryce. 2000. Slugs of Western Australia: A guide to the species from the Indian to West Pacific Oceans. Western Australian Museum. 184 pp.

Wee Y.C. and Peter K. L. Ng. 1994. A First Look at Biodiversity in Singapore. National Council on the Environment. 163pp.

Wee Yeow Chin. 1992. A Guide to Medicinal Plants. The Singapore Science Centre. 160pp

Wee Yeow Chin. 1983. Ferns of Singapore. The Singapore Science Centre. 72pp.

Wee Yeow Chin. 2003. Tropical Trees and Shrubs: A Selection for Urban Plantings. Sun Tree Publishing. 392pp.

Wood, Elizabeth & Michael Aw, 2002. Fishes, Corals and Invertebrates of Malaysia and the South China Sea. New Holland Publishers, UK. 144 pp.
